title: Array.reduce() calls should include an initial value
description: "Array.reduce()" calls should include an initial valueAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalreliabilityBugtype-dependentWhy is this an issue?More InfoTheArray.prototype.reduce()method in JavaScript is used to apply a function against an accumulator and each element in the array (from left to right) to reduce it to a single output value. It is a convenient method that can simplify logic in your code.However, it’s important to always provide an initial value as the second argument toreduce(). The initial value is used as the first argument to the first call of the callback function.
recommendation: Always provide an initial value as the second argument to reduce().
impact: Potential runtime errors with empty arrays

noncompliant: const sum = numbers.reduce((acc, val) => acc + val); // No initial value
const result = arr.reduce(callback); // Missing initial value
data.reduce((prev, curr) => prev.concat(curr)); // No initial value
compliant: const sum = numbers.reduce((acc, val) => acc + val, 0); // With initial value
const result = arr.reduce(callback, []); // With initial value
data.reduce((prev, curr) => prev.concat(curr), []); // With initial value

checks.message: Always provide an initial value as the second argument to reduce().
